content.problemStatement:
There were NNN students (numbered 111 through NNN) participating in the Indian Programming Camp (IPC) and they watched a total of KKK lectures (numbered 111 through KKK). For each student iii and each lecture jjj, the iii-th student watched the jjj-th lecture for Ti,jT_{i, j}Ti,j‚Äã minutes.

Additionally, for each student iii, we know that this student asked the question, "What is the criteria for getting a certificate?" QiQ_iQi‚Äã times.

The criteria for getting a certificate is that a student must have watched at least MMM minutes of lectures in total and they must have asked the question no more than 101010 times.

Find out how many participants are eligible for a certificate.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains K+1K+1K+1 space-separated integers Ti,1,Ti,2,‚Ä¶,Ti,K,QiT_{i, 1}, T_{i, 2}, \ldots, T_{i, K}, Q_iTi,1‚Äã,Ti,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ti,K‚Äã,Qi‚Äã.
content.outputFormat:
Print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of participants eligible for a certificate.

###Constraints


1‚â§N,K‚â§1,0001 \le N, K \le 1,0001‚â§N,K‚â§1,000
1‚â§M‚â§1061 \le M \le 10^61‚â§M‚â§106
1‚â§Qi‚â§1061 \le Q_i \le 10^61‚â§Qi‚Äã‚â§106 for each valid iii
1‚â§Ti,j‚â§1,0001 \le T_{i, j} \le 1,0001‚â§Ti,j‚Äã‚â§1,000 for each valid iii and jjj

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Participant 111 watched 1+2+1+2=61 + 2 + 1 + 2 = 61+2+1+2=6 minutes of lectures and asked the question 555 times. Since 6<M6 \lt M6<M, this participant does not receive a certificate.
Participant 222 watched 3+5+1+3=123 + 5 + 1 + 3 = 123+5+1+3=12 minutes of lectures and asked the question 444 times. Since 12‚â•M12 \ge M12‚â•M and 4‚â§104 \le 104‚â§10, this participant receives a certificate.
Participant 333 watched 1+2+4+5=121 + 2 + 4 + 5 = 121+2+4+5=12 minutes of lectures and asked the question 111111 times. Since 12‚â•M12 \ge M12‚â•M but 11>1011 \gt 1011>10, this participant does not receive a certificate.
Participant 444 watched 1+1+1+3=61 + 1 + 1 + 3 = 61+1+1+3=6 minutes of lectures and asked the question 121212 times. Since 6<M6 \lt M6<M and 12>1012 \gt 1012>10, this participant does not receive a certificate.

Only participant 222 receives a certificate.
